about.title: About WhereHoopz
about.intro: From basketball lovers, for basketball lovers. WhereHoopz is a community-driven platform that helps you discover basketball courts near you and connect with fellow hoopers in your area.
about.intro2: Whether you're looking for a quick solo session or a competitive pickup game, we make it easy to find courts, see real-time player activity, and join the action. Our mission is to bring hoopers together and make every court feel like home.
about.findCourtsDesc: Discover basketball hoops in your neighborhood with our interactive map. See court conditions, photos, and details before you go.
about.letOthersKnowDesc: Heading to a court? Let other hoopers know you'll be there. Find pickup games and never shoot alone again.
about.connectHoopersDesc: Meet new basketball buddies in your area. Build your local hooping community and find regular playing partners.
about.tipText: Enable location services for the best experience. This helps us show you the nearest courts and calculate distances accurately.

privacy.title: Privacy & Data Policy
privacy.intro: We take your privacy seriously. This page explains what data we collect, how we use it, and the choices you have about your information.
privacy.whatWeCollectText: When you add a basketball court, we collect the details you submit including photos, description, location coordinates, and court condition. We also store metadata such as the submission date. If you enable location services, we temporarily access your location to show nearby courts and calculate distances — this data is not stored on our servers.
privacy.howWeUseText: Court information you submit is visible to all users to help the community find places to play. We may use aggregated, anonymized data to improve the service and understand usage patterns. We do not sell your personal information to third parties.
privacy.imagesLicensingText: By uploading photos of basketball courts, you confirm you have the right to share them. Images are displayed publicly on the site to help other users identify courts. If you need an image removed, contact us and we will handle your request promptly.
privacy.locationDataText: WhereHoopz uses your device location (with your permission) to show nearby courts and calculate distances. This location data is processed locally in your browser and is not stored on our servers. You can disable location access at any time through your browser settings.
privacy.dataRetentionText: You can request deletion of any court data you've submitted by contacting us. Requests are processed within a reasonable timeframe. Some aggregated statistics may remain in anonymized form.
privacy.contactText: For privacy requests or questions, please visit our

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: Have questions, feedback, or found a bug? We'd love to hear from you!
contact.suggestCourtText: Know a basketball court that's not on our community's map? Use the
contact.MarkTakeCare: I will review your suggestion and add the verification badge as soon as possible.

faq.title: How to Use WhereHoopz
faq.intro: Find answers to common questions about using WhereHoopz.

readyToPlay.question: How do I let others know I'm going to play?
readyToPlay.answer:
On any court card (on the home page or courts list), tap the green "Ready to play" button. This will navigate you to the court's page, then use the enrollment form to sign up for a session. Choose your arrival time, how long you'll play, and whether you're open to playing with others. Your enrollment will appear on the court's "Who's Playing" panel, letting other hoopers know you'll be there.

Note: you can have one enrollment per court for today and one for a future date.

addCourt.question: How do I add a new basketball court?
addCourt.answer: Tap "Add Hoop" in the navigation under the menu. Fill in the court name, drag the marker on the map to set the location (or use your current location), add a description, select the condition, court type (indoor/outdoor), and court access (gratis/paid), then upload at least one photo. The star icon lets you choose which photo appears as the main profile image. A progress bar at the top tracks how many of the 6 required fields you've completed.

takePhoto.question: How do I take a good court photo?
takePhoto.answer: For the best results: 1) Include the hoop and backboard clearly in the shot. 2) Show the court surface so others can see the condition. 3) Take photos during daylight for better visibility. 4) Capture any unique features like lighting, benches, or multiple hoops. 5) Avoid photos with people's faces for privacy.

verified.question: What does the green verified badge mean?
verified.answer: The green verified badge means that an admin has personally confirmed the court details are accurate — either by visiting the court in person or by verifying the submitted information. Courts without the badge are community-submitted and may not yet have been reviewed.

faq.conditionTitle: How do I rate a court's condition?
faq.conditionIntro: When adding a court, select the condition that best matches what you see:

excellent.title: Excellent
excellent.description: Like new or recently renovated. Smooth, crack-free surface. Net in perfect condition. Clear court markings. Well-maintained backboard and rim.

good.title: Good
good.description: Well-maintained with minor wear. Surface has minimal cracks or rough spots. Net may have slight wear but works fine. Court markings visible. Backboard and rim fully functional.

fair.title: Fair
fair.description: Playable but showing age. Some cracks or uneven spots on the surface. Net may be worn or missing. Faded court markings. Equipment works but could use maintenance.

poor.title: Poor
poor.description: Needs significant repairs. Major cracks, holes, or debris on surface. Net missing or badly damaged. No visible markings. Bent rim or damaged backboard. Still playable but with caution.

faq.courtTypeIntro: When adding a court, select the type that matches the venue:

outdoor.title: Outdoor
outdoor.description: An open-air court with no roof or walls. Exposed to weather conditions. Common in parks, playgrounds, and public spaces.

indoor.title: Indoor
indoor.description: A court inside a building or covered structure. Protected from weather. Typically found in gyms, sports halls, and recreation centers.
